topic_summary: US naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz

The Senate voted 47-52 for the fourth time this year to reject a War Powers Resolution that would require President Trump to withdraw U.S. forces from hostilities against Iran, even as the conflict approaches its 60-day statutory deadline and ceasefire negotiations with Iran collapsed in Islamabad.
proposition: Congress must invoke the War Powers Act to force the Trump administration to seek authorization for its military campaign against Iran.

topic_summary: President Trump signed an executive order on March 31, 2026 directing the U.S. Postal Service to deliver mail-in ballots only to voters on federally verified citizenship lists, triggering lawsuits from more than two dozen states and intensifying national debate heading into the November midterm elections.
proposition: Trump's executive order restricting mail-in voting is a necessary and legitimate step to protect election integrity.

topic_summary: On April 12, 2026, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban conceded a landslide election defeat to opposition leader Peter Magyar, whose Tisza party won a supermajority in parliament, ending Orban's 16-year rule and sending political shockwaves through Washington, Moscow, and Brussels.
proposition: Viktor Orban's election defeat is a warning sign for the MAGA movement and its model of nationalist populism.

topic_summary: Congress this week failed to pass an 18-month clean extension of FISA Section 702, the warrantless foreign surveillance law set to expire April 20, ultimately passing only a 10-day stopgap after a bloc of twenty House Republicans joined Democrats in demanding warrant protections for Americans' communications swept up in the program.
proposition: Section 702 of FISA should be reauthorized without additional warrant requirements.

topic_summary: During Tax Week, President Trump traveled to Las Vegas to promote the One Big Beautiful Bill Act's no-tax-on-tips and no-tax-on-overtime provisions, while Democrats condemned the law on Tax Day for pairing temporary worker tax breaks with permanent cuts for the wealthy and nearly one trillion dollars in Medicaid reductions.
proposition: Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill Act has delivered meaningful tax relief to working Americans and should be defended heading into the November midterms.

topic_summary: On April 15, 2026, thirteen House Democrats introduced six articles of impeachment against Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, accusing him of waging unauthorized war against Iran, targeting civilians including a girls' school in Minab, gross negligence with classified information, obstructing congressional oversight, and abusing the powers of his office.
proposition: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth should be removed from office for his conduct of the Iran war.

topic_summary: Pope Leo XIV, the first American-born pope, publicly condemned the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran and called Trump's threat to annihilate Iranian civilization 'truly unacceptable,' prompting President Trump to attack the pontiff on Truth Social as 'WEAK on Crime' and 'terrible for Foreign Policy,' igniting a sweeping international backlash.
proposition: The United States government should respond to Pope Leo XIV's remarks condemning aggressive war with diplomacy rather than sharp personal rebuke.
